item.title: Topolski's Chronicle, V.3 No.1, 1955
item.creator: Topolski, Feliks, 1907-1989
item.description: Topolski's Chronicle is a series of broadsheets created by Feliks Topolski and distributed bimonthly to subscribers from 1953 through 1982. Topolski was a superlative draughtsman and covered current events with an artistic, unique style.

pages.typeset_text:
UNIVERSITY OF UTAH LIBRARIES
TOPOLSKI’S CHRONICLE No. 1 (37) Vol. III 1955
...nothing differeth and therefore never tweth... Sir Philip Sidney (1580)
375
THE POPE’S ANNUAL MESSAGE
...Only spiritual ideas “acquired now for many centuries as part of the tradition of Christian Europe can sustain comparison—and, moreover emerge victorious, to the extent that these ideas are really lived—with the false idea, concrete and effective though it is, which apparently holds together the other group in cohesion . . .” . . . There are “millions in both camps in whom the imprint of Christ is preserved in a more or less active degree”, and they, too, no less than the faithful and fervent believers, should be called upon to collaborate towards a renewed basis of unity for the human race...
sketch_descriptions: Loose, abstract black ink drawing suggesting swirling forms; possibly representing a human face or figures engaged in expressive movement, with thick, looping lines dominating the central area of the page.
pages.page_summary: This page from Topolski's Chronicle features an abstract sketch and highlights a reflective passage from the Pope's annual message, emphasizing spiritual unity and the tradition of Christian Europe.

pages.typeset_text:
The Song of Business by John Ney
There is a Song and it is the Song of Business
It is a Song of the first, the second, and third things
of Commerce and profit and loss
and it is a Song of desire and measure of things
A Song of tasks for gain and reward and exchange and money
it is a song of the spaces I walked in the world
that I measured and counted and scanned.
And the Song of Business is blended in the words
I write and the actions I take
It is the Song of Business—and I invent the song—as
I go—and as I join hands with thousands
We command forces in a new world
We trade over distances that were unmarked,
We scheme and we build
We buy and we sell and we borrow and lend
and we tally and reckon and plan as we go.
Aye! We are the Builders of the Gadgets
We are the makers and movers and pushers and planners
We possess the will and the brains
We travel the Roads of the Gadgets
and the bridges and tunnels and tubes
There is no POSSIBLE limit.
And they prospered according to their participation
and each participated
AND WAS ACCOUNTABLE
And all worked, strove, planned, and fulfilled tasks
according to the measure of time and effort
And the Road called Business SNAPPED!
And I selected from the crowd one hundred women
and one hundred men
and they accepted my invitation
And the number was even—
And the reward was even—
But the work was not even—
For the time and force that attended the tasks was not even
And the increments were not even
My song was not even
And I reckoned my failure:
The recompense of Business is not even—
It is a distribution of abundance aneath luck of the
heads and the hands
And certain men and women make fortunes
while others sweat and dream and starve.
And a few die young from the weight of Business
and others die old from the load of Business
But all are bound to the logic of expectations
and the wheel goes round with the oil of hope
And the wheel goes round with the fuel of greed
And the wheel goes round with the tears of defeat
And the wheel goes round with the laughter of success
And at the end of the year I had asked about the
future plans of those about me
and I counted their aspirations
and I knew what each wished to do and be
for the next year.
And the increments were not even but I added them
all up, and the sum was dazzling.
If the aspirations of men and women were coins
and could be minted by States,
then each year the Treasury would be full
and every desire would blossom in Springtime
But many would spend the increments of nightless
days and sleepless nights
on the ships and roads and farms of Business
and not all the harvests are bright.
And each year the warriors train with their swords
And the traders count with their ledgers
and the stevedores sweat with their loads
But the coin falls uneven across the world.
But all dreams have their reward and the fertility
of men and women is Rest and Laughter
and the harvest is FOR CREATION.
And this year there were eight hundred and
fifty-six thousand three hundred and eight
And the reward of dreams and the sustenance of
actions
Was computed and tabulated and traded
And the measure was profit and the loss
was recorded.
And for endless thousands who made the number of
eight hundred fifty-six thousand three hundred and
eight
the season was failure so that the few
might reap a profit in rude abundance.
And the count was not even
For though the number was the same each year
And the count was eight hundred fifty means of that year
there were a hundred thousand and one
whose means had changed
by a hair or a fortune
So the ledger was always revised
And you take the fifteen years into the sum
And the count was fourteen million three hundred
forty-one thousand two hundred and thirty-two
And every year the increment was compiled
The Song of Business is not even
a spreadsheet of parallel lines
But a web—
And yet this year the total was eight hundred and
fifty six thousand three hundred and eight
although the losers of last year were not the
losers of this year
And the winners change from year to year
And the total remains unequal for the losers,
and for the winners the total is more than equal
And the Tally of Business is not for Justice
though many men talk of Justice and pray to Justice
And the wheel goes round with the lie of age
and the lie of profit
and the lie of loss
And there were five thousand two hundred
houses built on clay foundations
and thirty five thousand four hundred and sixteen
on Rock
and two hundred seventy nine on the sands of expectation
and none in the shade of the Tree of Life
For the Builders did not pause to measure the soils
And the men and women who made the Edicts
and the men who signed the drafts of these
enterprises
And the men and women who designed the plans and
paid the advances
And all the force and logic was the force and logic
of reward and achievement
reward and achievement
And the wheel goes round with the circular
lusts of men
And the count was made, dollar and cent
And the count was made, leverage on leverage
The cost was tallied in human flesh and hope
The gains were entered in the ledger of the song
and the cost was hidden in the injury of losers
and the profit was mirrored in the shine of the gainers
But the cost—the cost—the cost—
And I had THOUGHT
about the Victory
And I contemplated the laughter
And I measured the charade
And I added—
And lost—
And the futility was greater than the profit
But the wheel goes round
round and round
and the labor machine never stops
And the blueprint of Business is never without cloud
But the harvest is always rich
And I saw just as the wheel turned
And the cost was MARKED
And the gain was MARKED
And the flesh and bone of the song
was written in ledgers
And the poem became a balance sheet
And the fortune was marked by the weight of the cost
But the Song of Business
is the victory of Creation
The Song of Business is measured in struggle
And it is tallied in the increments
of time and motion
And it is marked
by every brother's work
It is measured by every woman's dream
It is counted by every worker's skill
It is inventoried by every machine's speed
It is weighed in the balances
of the strong and the weak
It is counted by the increments of the stars
And its song is the Song of Business
It is the Song of men and women who strove
to build
It is the Song of risk
It is the Song of hope
It is the Song of expectation
It is the Song of Life
It is the Song of Business
The Song of Creation
No apologies are needed here, The CHRONICLE promised in its first issue, one year and a half ago, to incorporate textual contributions which may be complementary to the draughtsman's survey. From John Ney's letter: 'you left me a loophole in saying: "If you are inclined to employ a short auto-biography..." I am not so inclined. As to the epilogue, I am 31 and do come from St. Louis, Missouri.'
sketch_descriptions: A wide, horizontal, expressive drawing of two figures lying side by side, rendered in dark, bold lines, stretches across the top margin of the page, intersected by two vertical red bars.
At the bottom center, a loose, gestural portrait of a man's head and face, possibly intended as an authorial or editorial figure, rendered in black line with a raw, direct style.
pages.page_summary: This page features the full text of the poem 'The Song of Business' by John Ney, accompanied by expressive sketches by Felix Topolski. The poem explores the nature of commerce, work, reward, and human aspiration, while the artwork underscores its reflective, social themes.

pages.typeset_text:
PANDIT NEHRU, on relinquishing the Presidency of the Congress Party, declared that he will press “towards a socialistic pattern of society in India, along the path of peaceful evolution”

'TOPOLSKI'S CHRONICLE' IS PUBLISHED 24 TIMES A YEAR AT 2s 6d. A COPY
YEARLY SUBSCRIPTION RATES (WITH FOLDER AND INDEX) TO ANY PART OF THE WORLD ARE A. L3. 5. 0 (or equivalent); SHORTER PERIODS PRO RATA. AIR MAIL BY QUOTATION.
SUBSCRIPTIONS FROM OUTSIDE THE STERLING AREA MAY BE PAID THROUGH THE UNESCO COUPON SCHEME.

Handprinted in Great Britain by C. Bednarczuk, 146 Sutton Walk, S.E.1 from blocks made by Austin Miles Ltd and published from Bridge Arch 158, Sutton Walk, South Bank, London, S.E.1 Copyright reserved.
sketch_descriptions: Expressive black ink portrait of an elderly bald man with intense eyes, rendered in energetic, gestural lines on a brown paper background.

events: relinquishing the Presidency of the Congress Party

pages.page_summary: This page features Felix Topolski's vivid portrait of Pandit Nehru, accompanied by Nehru's statement on his vision for India's society upon leaving the presidency of the Congress Party. Editorial and publishing details for Topolski's Chronicle appear at the bottom.
